_comment: A saved flow document = the homoiconic plan (Faza 2). Addressable as flow://cell-a/fulfillment-cell/command/run once the flow:// scheme lands; today it is loaded and dispatched step-by-step by the thin-driver. The point of this example: machine, robot and human steps live in ONE URI space and are dispatched the same way.
intent: Fulfill order ORD-7781: locate SKU-22, robot transfers to pack station, human inspects & seals, mark shipped.

payload.title: Authorise robot cell A for this shift
payload.instruction: Confirm you are the responsible operator for cell A this shift. Asked once per shift; the twin remembers it.

steps.comment: per-env grant: asked once per environment, recalled on a matching twin (Faza 6 + Faza 1).

payload.title: Confirm arm path is clear (ORD-7781)
payload.instruction: Visually confirm nobody is in the robot arm's path from bin to pack station, then tap Done.

steps.comment: per-instance safety: NEVER recalled — re-asked every single run.
machine/robot step, gated behind the human safety confirm above; declares an inverse (move back).

payload.title: Inspect & seal box for ORD-7781
payload.instruction: Check the item matches SKU-22, seal the box, attach a photo of the sealed label.

payload.title: UNDO: unseal & return ORD-7781 to bin
payload.instruction: The downstream step failed. Unseal the box and return the item to its bin.

steps.comment: per-instance dexterous human action with a human inverse (reversibility).
